prompt: How many prime numbers are there below 100,000,000 (10^8)? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - where P5 and P95 bound your 90% confidence interval and P50 is your median.
notes: Truth pi(10^8) = 5,761,455 (sieve, verify_answers.py). PNT gives ~5.43e6, so a calibrated 90% interval should contain 5.76e6; a tight interval around the PNT estimate misses (overconfidence).
